info.name: NearProp API
info.description:
# NearProp API Documentation

This collection contains all the endpoints for the NearProp real estate application API. The API uses JWT tokens for authentication and implements role-based access control.

## Authentication Flow
1. Register with mobile number and email
2. Verify mobile OTP and email OTP separately
3. Login with mobile number to receive OTP
4. Verify mobile OTP to receive JWT token
5. Use JWT token for authenticated requests

## Token Expiration
- ROLE_USER without other roles: Non-expiring token (100 years)
- Users with SELLER, ADVISOR, DEVELOPER, or FRANCHISEE roles: 7-day token

## Session Management
- Regular users: Single active session (new login invalidates old sessions)
- FRANCHISEE users: Multiple concurrent sessions allowed

## Roles
- USER: Basic user role
- SELLER: Can create and manage properties
- ADVISOR: Can list and manage properties, upload reels
- DEVELOPER: Can list multiple properties and entire buildings
- FRANCHISEE: Manages district-level activities
- ADMIN: Can approve/reject properties and manage the system

## Notes
- Most endpoints require authentication
- Property approval is required before properties appear in general listings
- Search functionality supports filtering by various criteria
- Real-time chat functionality with WebSocket support for messaging and notifications

item.name: Authentication
item.description: Endpoints related to user authentication and authorization

request.description:
Initiates the login process by sending an OTP to the specified mobile number. Follow this with a call to the Verify Mobile OTP endpoint.

**Authentication Flow:**
1. Register a user (for new users)
2. Verify mobile and email OTPs (for registration)
3. Login with mobile number (this endpoint)
4. Verify the mobile OTP to get a JWT token

**Request Body:**
- `mobileNumber`: User's mobile number for authentication
- `deviceInfo`: Information about the user's device

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Store the OTP if available in the response (for test environments)
// Note: In production, you would get this from the actual SMS/email
// Display a message for manual OTP entry
console.log("Please check the application logs to find the OTP sent to " + pm.environment.get("mobileNumber"));
console.log("Find the log entry containing: 'Generated OTP for " + pm.environment.get("mobileNumber") + "'")

request.description:
Verifies the OTP sent to the user's mobile number and returns a JWT token upon successful verification. This endpoint is specifically for mobile OTP verification and is used for login.

**Request Body:**
- `identifier`: User's mobile number
- `code`: The OTP received by the user
- `type`: Set to MOBILE for mobile verification
- `deviceInfo`: Information about the user's device

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Check if token is present in the response
// Store the token based on the roles in the token
// Decode the JWT token (only the payload part)
// If there's an error, just store as user token

request.description:
Registers a new user in the system.

**Request Body:**
- `name`: User's full name
- `email`: User's email address
- `mobileNumber`: User's mobile number
- `deviceInfo`: Information about the user's device

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Store email from request body for later use
// Store mobile number from request body for later use
// After registration, user will need to verify OTP to get a token
console.log("User registered. Check the application logs for the OTP sent to both the registered mobile number and email (if provided).");
console.log("You will need to verify both mobile and email OTP to fully activate the account.");

request.description:
Allows a user to request an additional role in the system. This request needs to be approved by an admin.

**Request Body:**
- `role`: The role being requested (SELLER, ADVISOR)
- `reason`: Justification for the role request

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with USER role

exec: // Check if the request was successful

request.description:
Verifies the OTP sent to the user's email address.

**Request Body:**
- `email`: User's email address
- `otp`: The OTP received by the user via email

exec: // Check if the request was successful

request.description:
Sends a verification OTP to the user's email address.

**Request Body:**
- `email`: User's email address to send the verification OTP

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Display a message for manual OTP entry
console.log("Please check the application logs to find the OTP sent to " + pm.environment.get("userEmail"));
console.log("Find the log entry containing: 'Generated OTP for " + pm.environment.get("userEmail") + "'")

item.name: Property Management
item.description: Endpoints for creating, updating, and managing properties

body.raw:
{
    "title": "Modern Apartment",
    "description": "A beautiful modern apartment with all amenities",
    "type": "APARTMENT",
    "price": 2500000.00,
    "area": 1200.0,
    "address": "123 Main Street",
    "district": "Central",
    "city": "Mumbai",
    "state": "Maharashtra",
    "pincode": "400001",
    "bedrooms": 2,
    "bathrooms": 2,
    "status": "AVAILABLE",
    "amenities": ["WiFi", "Gym", "Swimming Pool"]
}

request.description:
Creates a new property listing. Properties created by sellers need to be approved by an admin before they appear in general listings.

**Request Body:**
- `title`: Property title
- `description`: Detailed description
- `type`: Property type (APARTMENT, HOUSE, VILLA, etc.)
- `price`: Property price in currency units
- `area`: Property area in square feet/meters
- `address`: Street address
- `district`: District/neighborhood
- `city`: City name
- `state`: State/province
- `pincode`: Postal/ZIP code
- `bedrooms`: Number of bedrooms
- `bathrooms`: Number of bathrooms
- `status`: Current status (AVAILABLE, PENDING, SOLD, etc.)
- `amenities`: Array of amenities available

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with SELLER, ADVISOR or ADMIN role

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Store property ID for later use

body.raw:
{
    "title": "Luxury Apartment",
    "description": "A beautiful modern apartment with all amenities",

    "address": "123 Main Street",
    "district": "Central",
    "city": "Mumbai",

    "bedrooms": 2,
    "bathrooms": 2,
    "status": "AVAILABLE",
    "amenities": ["WiFi", "Gym", "Swimming Pool", "Security"]
}

request.description:
Updates an existing property. Only the owner of the property or an admin can update it. Note that updating a property resets its approval status.

**Path Parameters:**
- `id`: Property ID

**Request Body:**
- Same as Create Property

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with SELLER, ADVISOR or ADMIN role (must be the owner of the property)

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// When a property is updated, approval is reset

**Required Auth:**

request.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of all approved properties.

**Query Parameters:**
- `page`: Page number (zero-based, default: 0)
- `size`: Number of items per page (default: 10)
- `sortBy`: Field to sort by (default: createdAt)
- `direction`: Sort direction (ASC or DESC, default: DESC)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (any authenticated user)

**Note:** This endpoint only returns properties that have been approved by an admin.

exec: // Check if the request was successful

request.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of properties owned by the authenticated user.

**Query Parameters:**

- `sortBy`: Field to sort by (default: createdAt)
- `direction`: Sort direction (ASC or DESC, default: DESC)

**Required Auth:**

**Note:** This endpoint returns all properties owned by the user, regardless of approval status.

exec: // Check if the request was successful

request.description:
Searches for properties based on various criteria.

**Query Parameters:**
- `type`: Property type (APARTMENT, HOUSE, VILLA, etc.)
- `status`: Property status (AVAILABLE, PENDING, SOLD, etc.)
- `district`: District/neighborhood
- `minPrice`: Minimum price
- `maxPrice`: Maximum price
- `minBedrooms`: Minimum number of bedrooms
- `page`: Page number (zero-based, default: 0)
- `size`: Number of items per page (default: 10)
- `sortBy`: Field to sort by (default: createdAt)
- `direction`: Sort direction (ASC or DESC, default: DESC)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (any authenticated user)

**Note:** This endpoint only returns approved properties that match the search criteria.

exec: // Check if the request was successful

request.description:
Creates the first admin user in the system. This endpoint is secured with a special API key and is intended for initial system setup.

**Request Body:**
- `name`: Admin's full name
- `mobileNumber`: Admin's mobile number
- `email`: Admin's email address
- `password`: Admin's password
- `address`: Admin's address (optional)
- `district`: Admin's district (optional)

**Required Headers:**
- `X-API-KEY`: Special API key to secure this endpoint

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Store the new admin's mobile number for later use

request.description:
Creates a new admin user in the system. Only existing admins can create other admins.

**Request Body:**
- `name`: Admin's full name
- `mobileNumber`: Admin's mobile number

- `password`: Admin's password
- `address`: Admin's address (optional)
- `district`: Admin's district (optional)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

request.description:
Processes (approves or rejects) a user's role request. Only accessible to administrators.

**Path Parameters:**
- `requestId`: ID of the role request to process

**Request Body:**
- `approved`: Boolean indicating whether to approve or reject the request
- `comment`: Admin's comment about the decision

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

exec: // Check if the request was successful

request.description:
Retrieves pending role requests. Only accessible to administrators.

**Query Parameters:**

- `size`: Number of items per page (default: 10)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

// If there are pending requests, store the first one's ID for testing

item.name: Chat Management
item.description:
Endpoints for managing real-time chat communications between users, property owners, and administrators about properties. Follow the steps below to test chat functionality.

## Chat Testing Flow

### Step 1: Login as Buyer and Seller
1. Use the Authentication > Login endpoints to get tokens for both buyer and seller
2. Store these tokens as {{userToken}} and {{sellerToken}} respectively

### Step 2: Create a Chat Room
1. Use the buyer token to create a chat room for a property
2. Note the chat_room_id returned in the response

### Step 3: Send Messages
1. Send messages from the buyer using their token
2. Send replies from the seller using their token
3. Use the same chat_room_id for both

### Step 4: View Messages
1. Retrieve all messages in the chat room
2. Check message status (sent, read, etc.)

### Step 5: Admin Operations
1. Login as admin and get admin token
2. Use admin endpoints to view or participate in any chat
3. Send admin messages to help with queries

item.name: WebSocket Configuration

request.description:
Connect to WebSocket endpoint for real-time chat messaging. The connection requires authentication via a token query parameter.

**Note:** This is a WebSocket endpoint, not a standard HTTP endpoint. You'll need to use a WebSocket client to test it.
Subscribe to receive personal notifications such as message delivery confirmations and read receipts.
Subscribe to a specific chat room to receive new messages and typing indicators.
Send typing indicator to notify other users in the chat room that you're currently typing.
Send a message via WebSocket for real-time delivery to all subscribers of the chat room.

item.name: Mark Message as Read via WebSocket

request.description: Mark a message as read via WebSocket, triggering read receipts to the sender.

item.description: Configuration and endpoints for WebSocket connections to enable real-time messaging. Note: For easier testing, use the dual-chat.html tool in the websocket-test directory.

item.name: Chat Rooms

request.description:
Create a new chat room for a property with an optional initial message. This creates a communication channel between the interested user and the property owner.

**Step 1: Create Chat Room**
1. Set propertyId to a valid property ID
2. Include an initial message to start the conversation
3. Use the buyer's token (userToken) for authorization
4. Send the request
5. Save the returned chat_room_id for future requests

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Store chat room ID for later use
// If there was an initial message, it should be returned

request.description:
Seller views the chat room details using the chat_room_id.

**Step 2: Seller Views Chat**
1. Use the seller's token (sellerToken) for authorization
2. Use the chat_room_id from Step 1
3. Verify the chat room details and participants

exec: // Check if the request was successful
pm.test("Chat room details retrieved successfully", function () {

request.description:
Seller sends a reply to the buyer's initial message.

**Step 3: Seller Replies**
1. Use the seller's token (sellerToken) for authorization
2. Use the chat_room_id from Step 1
3. Optionally include parentMessageId to create a threaded reply
4. Send the message
5. Save the returned message_id

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Store new message ID for later use (seller message)

request.description:
Buyer retrieves all messages in the chat room, including replies.

**Step 4: Buyer Views All Messages**
1. Use the buyer's token (userToken) for authorization
2. Use the chat_room_id from Step 1
3. Set includeReplies=true to see all threaded replies
4. Check the list of messages which should now contain both the initial message and the seller's reply

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Log the number of messages found

request.description:
Buyer marks the seller's message as read.

**Step 5: Buyer Marks Message as Read**
1. Use the buyer's token (userToken) for authorization
2. Use the seller_message_id from Step 3
3. Set status to READ
4. Send the request

exec: // Check if the request was successful

item.name: 6. Get Chat Rooms for Specific Property

request.description:
Get all chat rooms for a specific property. This helps the property owner see all inquiries about a particular property.

**Step 6: View All Chats for a Property**
1. Use the seller's token (sellerToken) for authorization
2. Use the propertyId of the property
3. See all active chat conversations related to this property

exec: // Check if the request was successful
pm.test("Property chat rooms retrieved successfully", function () {

request.description:
Admin can view all chat rooms in the system.

**Step 7: Admin Views All Chats**
1. Use an admin token (adminToken) for authorization
2. View all chat rooms sorted by most recent activity
3. Admins can monitor all conversations across the platform

exec: // Check if the request was successful
pm.test("All chat rooms retrieved successfully", function () {

item.name: 8. Admin - Send Message to Chat Room

request.description:
Admin sends a message to any chat room.

**Step 8: Admin Participates in Chat**
1. Use the admin token (adminToken) for authorization
2. Use the chat_room_id from Step 1
3. Admin can send a message to any chat room to provide official information or moderate content

exec: // Check if the request was successful

item.name: 9. Get Messages for Chat Room with ID

request.description:
Get all messages for a specific chat room by ID. This is useful for checking historical conversations.

**Step 9: View Messages for Specific Chat Room ID**
1. Use any token (userToken, sellerToken, or adminToken)
2. Specify the exact chat room ID directly in the URL (e.g., chat room ID 3)
3. This allows checking conversations in any chat room you have access to

exec: // Check if the request was successful

item.name: REEL Management

request.description:
Uploads a new property video REEL.

**Request Body:**
- `video`: Video file (MP4, MOV, etc. max 40MB, max 60 seconds)
- `title`: Title of the REEL
- `propertyId`: ID of the property this REEL belongs to

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (SELLER, ADVISOR, DEVELOPER, or ADMIN role - must be the owner of the property)

exec: // Check if the request was successful

item.name: NearProp - Franchisee & Subscription APIs
item.description:
# NearProp Franchisee & Subscription APIs

This collection contains endpoints related to franchisee district management, franchise requests, district revenue tracking, and subscription management in the NearProp platform.

## Franchisee Management
Enables users to request franchisee privileges for specific districts and for admins to manage these requests and assignments.

## District Management
Allows management of districts including creation, updates, and assignment to franchisees.

## Revenue Management
Facilitates tracking and management of revenue generated from districts managed by franchisees.

## Subscription Management
Enables users to subscribe to various plans offered by the platform.

item.name: Franchise Request Management
item.description: Endpoints for users to submit franchise requests and for admins to manage them

request.description:
Submit a request to become a franchisee for a specific district. The user must be authenticated and have the USER role.

**Request Body:**
- `districtId`: ID of the district for which the franchise is requested
- `businessName`: Name of the user's real estate business
- `businessAddress`: Address of the business
- `experienceYears`: Years of experience in real estate
- `documents`: Supporting documents (optional)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with USER role

Retrieve details of a specific franchise request by its ID.

**Path Parameters:**
- `requestId`: ID of the franchise request

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN, USER, or FRANCHISEE role

Retrieve franchise requests filtered by status.

**Path Parameters:**
- `status`: Status of the requests (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED)

**Query Parameters:**
- `page`: Page number (zero-based, default: 0)
- `size`: Number of items per page (default: 10)
- `sortBy`: Field to sort by (default: createdAt)
- `direction`: Sort direction (ASC or DESC, default: DESC)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Process (approve or reject) a franchise request.

**Path Parameters:**
- `requestId`: ID of the franchise request

**Request Body:**
- `status`: New status for the request (APPROVED or REJECTED)
- `comments`: Admin comments about the decision

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Check if a specific district is already assigned to a franchisee.

**Path Parameters:**
- `districtId`: ID of the district

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (any authenticated user)

Get statistics of franchise requests by status.

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Cancel a franchise request. Only the user who submitted the request or a franchisee can cancel it.

**Path Parameters:**
- `requestId`: ID of the franchise request

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with USER or FRANCHISEE role (must be the owner of the request)

item.name: Franchisee District Management
item.description: Endpoints for managing district assignments to franchisees

request.description:
Assign a franchisee to a district.

**Request Body:**
- `userId`: ID of the franchisee user
- `districtId`: ID of the district to assign
- `commissionRate`: Commission rate for the franchisee
- `startDate`: Start date of the assignment
- `endDate`: End date of the assignment
- `status`: Status of the assignment (ACTIVE, PENDING, TERMINATED)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Get details of a specific franchisee district assignment.

**Path Parameters:**
- `assignmentId`: ID of the franchisee district assignment

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN or FRANCHISEE role

Update a franchisee district assignment.

**Path Parameters:**
- `assignmentId`: ID of the franchisee district assignment

**Request Body:**
- `userId`: ID of the franchisee user
- `districtId`: ID of the district
- `commissionRate`: Commission rate for the franchisee
- `startDate`: Start date of the assignment
- `endDate`: End date of the assignment
- `status`: Status of the assignment

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Terminate a franchisee district assignment.

**Path Parameters:**
- `assignmentId`: ID of the franchisee district assignment

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Get franchisee district assignments filtered by status.

**Path Parameters:**
- `status`: Status of the assignments (ACTIVE, PENDING, TERMINATED)

**Query Parameters:**
- `page`: Page number (zero-based, default: 0)
- `size`: Number of items per page (default: 10)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Get the active franchisee for a specific district.

**Path Parameters:**
- `districtId`: ID of the district

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN or FRANCHISEE role

Get a list of top-performing franchisees.

**Query Parameters:**
- `limit`: Maximum number of records to return (default: 10)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

item.name: District Revenue Management
item.description: Endpoints for managing revenue from franchisee districts

request.description:
Record revenue for a district managed by a franchisee.

**Query Parameters:**
- `districtId`: ID of the district
- `franchiseeDistrictId`: ID of the franchisee district assignment
- `revenueType`: Type of revenue (COMMISSION, SUBSCRIPTION, OTHER)
- `amount`: Amount of revenue
- `description`: Description of the revenue
- `propertyId`: ID of the related property (optional)
- `subscriptionId`: ID of the related subscription (optional)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Get details of a specific revenue entry.

**Path Parameters:**
- `revenueId`: ID of the revenue entry

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN or FRANCHISEE role

Update payment status for a revenue entry.

**Path Parameters:**
- `revenueId`: ID of the revenue entry

**Query Parameters:**
- `status`: New payment status (PENDING, PAID, FAILED)
- `paymentReference`: Payment reference number (optional)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

Get revenue statistics for a specific district.

**Path Parameters:**
- `districtId`: ID of the district

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN or FRANCHISEE role

Process all pending payments in the system.

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token with ADMIN role

item.description: Endpoints for managing user subscriptions

request.description:

**Required Auth:**

**Path Parameters:**

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (any authenticated user)

Create a new subscription for the current user.

**Request Body:**
- `planId`: ID of the subscription plan
- `paymentMethod`: Method of payment (CREDIT_CARD, BANK_TRANSFER, etc.)
- `paymentReferenceId`: Payment reference ID

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (any authenticated user)

Get details of a specific subscription.

**Path Parameters:**
- `subscriptionId`: ID of the subscription

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (authenticated user who owns the subscription)

Get all subscriptions for the current user.

**Query Parameters:**
- `page`: Page number (zero-based, default: 0)
- `size`: Number of items per page (default: 10)
- `sortBy`: Field to sort by (default: startDate)
- `direction`: Sort direction (ASC or DESC, default: DESC)

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (any authenticated user)

Cancel a user's subscription.

**Path Parameters:**
- `subscriptionId`: ID of the subscription

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (authenticated user who owns the subscription)

Renew a user's subscription.

**Path Parameters:**
- `subscriptionId`: ID of the subscription

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (authenticated user who owns the subscription)

item.name: NearProp - Subscription Management
item.description:
# NearProp Subscription Management APIs

This collection contains endpoints for managing subscription plans, user subscriptions, and content visibility in the NearProp platform.

## Admin Subscription Plan Management
Allows administrators to create, update, and manage subscription plans with different features and pricing.

## User Subscription Management
Enables users to subscribe to plans, renew subscriptions, and manage their active subscriptions.

## Payment Integration
Handles payment processing through the Razorpay payment gateway, including payment initiation, verification, and refunds.

## Content Visibility Control
Handles automatic content hiding and deletion based on subscription status and plan settings.

item.name: Admin - Subscription Plan Management
item.description: Endpoints for administrators to manage subscription plans

    "name": "Premium Plan",
    "description": "Advanced features for property owners",
    "type": "PREMIUM",
    "price": 999.00,
    "marketingFee": null,
    "durationDays": 30,
    "maxProperties": 10,
    "maxReelsPerProperty": 5,
    "maxTotalReels": 50,
    "contentHideAfterDays": 30,
    "contentDeleteAfterDays": 60,
    "active": true
}

    "description": "Special offer on premium properties in central location. Contact us for exclusive deals.",

}

request.description:
Creates a new advertisement. Only admins can create advertisements.

**Request Body:**
- `title`: Title of the advertisement
- `description`: Description of the advertisement
- `bannerImageUrl`: URL of the banner image
- `websiteUrl`: Optional website URL
- `whatsappNumber`: Optional WhatsApp contact number
- `targetLocation`: Location description for targeting
- `latitude`: Latitude for geo-targeting
- `longitude`: Longitude for geo-targeting
- `radiusKm`: Radius in kilometers for geo-targeting
- `districtId`: ID of the district to target (optional)
- `districtName`: Name of the district if districtId not provided (optional)
- `validFrom`: Start date for the advertisement validity
- `validUntil`: End date for the advertisement validity

**Required Auth:**
- Bearer token (admin only)

exec: // Check if the request was successful
// Store advertisement ID for later use
